Worker heights (~1.75m) provide secondary reference for mid-field distances. The COLMAP scene extent of 8.2m with 19 registered cameras constrains the total scene volume, suggesting the crane mast is roughly mid-scene at 3-4m. Fisheye lens distortion was accounted for by focusing on central frame regions for size estimation. Perspective convergence lines toward the CMU wall and the vertical crane mast provide additional depth ordering confirmation.

vima_spatial_analysis.reasoning: Primary depth cues used: (1) apparent angular size of parked vehicles on ground (~4-5m long vehicles appearing very small) to estimate ~15-20m vertical drop to yard level; (2) tower crane angular size — typical 1.5m mast width appearing as ~10px in 640px wide frame gives roughly 80-100m slant distance; (3) COLMAP sparse reconstruction with scene_extent_m of 8.2m and 19 registered cameras confirms the local platform geometry is compact (~8m span), consistent with an elevated floor or scaffold deck. Confidence is moderate due to heavy forearm occlusion, solar overexposure in upper frame, and fisheye distortion complicating angular measurements.
